title: CornerClosureFeatureInput Object
description: This class defines the methods and properties that pertain to the definition of a corner closure feature.

methods.name: classType
methods.description: Static function that all classes support that returns the type of the class as a string. The returned string matches the string returned by the objectType property. For example if you have a reference to an object and you want to check if it's a SketchLine you can use myObject.objectType == fusion.SketchLine.classType().

methods.name: setThreeBendCornerClosure
methods.description: Sets the corner closure input with the values to be used to create a three-bend corner closure feature. Before using this method, the definitionType property should be examined to determine whether the corner is two-bend or three-bend, as this method is only applicable for three-bend corner closures.

methods.name: setTwoBendCornerClosure
methods.description: Sets the corner closure input with the values to be used to create a two-bend corner closure feature. Before using this method, the definitionType property should be examined to determine whether the corner is two-bend or three-bend, as this method is only applicable for two-bend corner closures.

properties.name: definitionType
properties.description: Gets the type of corner closure defined.

properties.name: dominantEdge
properties.description: Gets and sets the dominant edge for the corner closure   After setting the edge, the definitionType property should be examined to determine whether the corner is two-bend or three-bend, as after the setting another edge the type of corner might be altered.

properties.name: isValid
properties.description: Indicates if this object is still valid, i.e. hasn't been deleted or some other action done to invalidate the reference.

properties.name: objectType
properties.description: This property is supported by all objects in the API and returns a string that contains the full name (namespace::objecttype) describing the type of the object.   It's often useful to use this in combination with the classType method to see if an object is a certain type. For example: if obj.objectType == adsk.core.Point3D.classType():

properties.name: submissiveEdge
properties.description: Gets and sets the submissive edge for the corner closure   After setting the edge, the definitionType property should be examined to determine whether the corner is two-bend or three-bend, as after the setting another edge the type of corner might be altered.

classType.title: CornerClosureFeatureInput.classType Method
classType.description: Static function that all classes support that returns the type of the class as a string. The returned string matches the string returned by the objectType property. For example if you have a reference to an object and you want to check if it's a SketchLine you can use myObject.objectType == fusion.SketchLine.classType().
classType.return_description: Returns a string indicating the type of the object.

setThreeBendCornerClosure.title: CornerClosureFeatureInput.setThreeBendCornerClosure Method
setThreeBendCornerClosure.description: Sets the corner closure input with the values to be used to create a three-bend corner closure feature. Before using this method, the definitionType property should be examined to determine whether the corner is two-bend or three-bend, as this method is only applicable for three-bend corner closures.
setThreeBendCornerClosure.return_description: Returns true if defining the corner closure is successful.

parameters.name: parameters
parameters.description: A ThreeBendCornerClosureParameters object that encapsulates all the required parameters for creating a three-bend corner closure feature. This includes miter gap, alignment settings, bend transition type, relief shape, and relief radius options.

setTwoBendCornerClosure.title: CornerClosureFeatureInput.setTwoBendCornerClosure Method
setTwoBendCornerClosure.description: Sets the corner closure input with the values to be used to create a two-bend corner closure feature. Before using this method, the definitionType property should be examined to determine whether the corner is two-bend or three-bend, as this method is only applicable for two-bend corner closures.
setTwoBendCornerClosure.return_description: Returns true if defining the corner closure is successful.

parameters.name: parameters
parameters.description: A TwoBendCornerClosureParameters object that encapsulates all the required parameters for creating a two-bend corner closure feature. This includes miter gap, alignment settings, bend transition type, relief shape, relief size, and relief placement options.

definitionType.title: CornerClosureFeatureInput.definitionType Property
definitionType.description: Gets the type of corner closure defined.

syntax.python:
# Get the value of the property.
propertyValue = cornerClosureFeatureInput_var.definitionType

dominantEdge.title: CornerClosureFeatureInput.dominantEdge Property
dominantEdge.description: Gets and sets the dominant edge for the corner closure After setting the edge, the definitionType property should be examined to determine whether the corner is two-bend or three-bend, as after the setting another edge the type of corner might be altered.

syntax.python:
# Get the value of the property.
propertyValue = cornerClosureFeatureInput_var.dominantEdge

# Set the value of the property.
cornerClosureFeatureInput_var.dominantEdge = propertyValue
